name: AI Model Governance
note: Estimates based on Azure retail pricing. Actual costs depend on usage, region, reserved instances, and enterprise agreements.

services.name: Azure Machine Learning
services.purpose: Model registry and lifecycle management — model versioning, metadata tracking, lineage graphs, A/B deployment orchestration, champion-challenger evaluation, and automated retraining triggers

dev.note: Free tier workspace, model registry, basic compute

prod.note: Managed compute for drift monitoring, ~100 evaluation runs/day

enterprise.note: GPU compute for retraining pipelines, ~500 evaluations/day

services.name: Azure AI Foundry
services.purpose: Evaluation and prompt flow — automated model evaluation pipelines, prompt versioning, evaluation metric dashboards, red-team testing orchestration, and responsible AI assessments

prod.note: Multi-project hub, scheduled evaluations, prompt flow hosting

enterprise.note: Multi-hub, cross-team governance, automated red-team pipelines

services.name: Azure DevOps
services.purpose: MLOps pipeline automation — CI/CD for model deployments, approval gates for production promotion, pipeline-as-code for training/evaluation/deployment, and release management with rollback

services.name: Cosmos DB
services.purpose: Governance metadata store — model cards, approval records, drift detection history, evaluation results, lineage graphs, compliance audit trail, and policy violation logs

prod.note: Multi-partition, 50GB, change feed for downstream alerts

services.name: Azure Policy
services.purpose: Automated governance enforcement — policies requiring model cards before deployment, mandatory bias evaluation, drift monitoring activation, approved model registry checks, and deployment region restrictions

dev.note: Built-in Azure service, basic policy assignments

enterprise.note: Initiative bundles, exemption management, remediation tasks

services.name: Azure Functions
services.purpose: Drift detection engine — scheduled statistical comparisons between training and production data distributions, feature drift alerts, prediction drift monitoring, and automated retraining triggers

services.name: Key Vault
services.purpose: ML workspace secrets, model endpoint keys, Cosmos DB credentials, DevOps service principal secrets, and encryption keys for model artifacts

services.name: Application Insights
services.purpose: Model observability — prediction latency, drift score trends, evaluation metric history, approval workflow duration, policy compliance rates, and retraining frequency tracking

optimization_tips: Run drift detection on a sample (10-20%) of production data rather than the full stream — statistically valid for distribution comparison at a fraction of the compute cost
Use scheduled drift checks (hourly/daily) instead of real-time monitoring for non-critical models — reserve real-time drift detection for models with direct revenue or safety impact
Cache evaluation results by model version hash — re-evaluating the same model checkpoint against the same test set is wasteful and can be skipped with cached results
Use Azure DevOps free tier for dev/staging and add parallel jobs only for production pipelines — most governance workflows are sequential and don't benefit from parallelism
Implement model card templates that auto-populate from ML workspace metadata — reduces manual documentation overhead and ensures compliance without additional human cost
Use Cosmos DB serverless for dev/staging governance metadata — governance data is write-heavy during deployments but read-light between compliance audits
